name: General Tao's Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Rice wine or dry Sherry
2 Dried red chilies, cut in half lengthwise
1 tablespoon Coarsely chopped fresh orange peel -OR-
2 teaspoons Soaked and coarsely chopped dried citrus peel
1/4 teaspoon Finely ground roasted Sichuan peppercorns (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut boneless chicken breast into thin slices, about 2 inches long, cutting against the grain.
2. Place the sliced chicken in a bowl with the dark soy sauce, rice wine, ginger, cornstarch, and sesame oil. Mix well and let marinate for approximately 20 minutes.
3. Heat peanut oil in a wok or large skillet until very hot.
4. Remove the chicken from the marinade using a slotted spoon and add it to the pan. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until browned.
5. Drain the chicken in a colander or sieve, then pour off most of the oil, leaving about 1 tablespoon.
6. Reheat the pan over high heat and add the dried chilies. Stir-fry for 10 seconds.
7. Return the chicken to the pan with the remaining ingredients and stir-fry for 4 minutes, mixing well.
8. Serve immediately over white rice.
